memo_markdown:
## Decision

**BOTTLENECK_NOT_EQUITY** — over the next 24 months, qualified large-transformer and HVDC-converter capacity is a real physical constraint, but the public-equity case is not admitted because a material product-revenue-to-per-share free-cash-flow bridge and a defensible expectations baseline remain unresolved. Research only; no leverage; no automatic trading. Benchmark: MSCI ACWI.

## Funded demand

The payers are regulated transmission owners, utilities, and project developers. Spend is supported by regulated network investment and project procurement rather than a purely aspirational theme: annual grid investment needs to rise materially through 2030, while grid connection queues and replacement needs remain large. Failure conditions are permitting delays, cancelled or deferred transmission projects, demand uncertainty, and customer substitution toward lower-capex grid upgrades. [IEA transmission-grid analysis](https://www.iea.org/news/rising-component-prices-and-supply-chain-pressures-are-hindering-the-development-of-transmission-grid-infrastructure), [IEA Electricity 2026](https://www.iea.org/reports/electricity-2026/grids)

## System map

Demand owner → regulated planning/procurement → engineering and standards → qualified high-voltage equipment design → specialized winding/core/insulation and power-electronics manufacturing → factory testing and field commissioning → transmission availability.

Mandatory roles screened: owner (qualified equipment manufacturer), unlocker (test capacity, workforce, components), substitute (dynamic ratings, topology optimisation, reconductoring, storage, voltage uprating), tollbooth (standards/qualification), absorber (utilities and EPCs bearing delay/cost), and public proxy. The constraint node is qualified factory capacity plus testing/engineering execution, not generic electrical demand.

## Constraint proof

The IEA reports procurement of large power transformers can take up to four years and direct-current cables over five years; transformer prices rose about 75% in real terms since 2019. Qualification, bespoke engineering, testing and concurrent global projects make rapid substitution difficult. [IEA](https://www.iea.org/news/rising-component-prices-and-supply-chain-pressures-are-hindering-the-development-of-transmission-grid-infrastructure)

This is not a permanent scarcity claim. The documented negative search found public factory-expansion support, workforce additions, grid-enhancing alternatives and permitting as countervailing paths. The strongest alternative causal explanation is that permitting and project execution—not equipment availability—remain the binding driver of delivered grid capacity and shareholder returns. The falsifier is sustained independent evidence of sub-18-month large-transformer lead times before product-level monetisation is disclosed. [DOE capacity records](https://www.energy.gov/cmei/manufacturing/applicant-self-disclosed-48c-projects), [IEA alternatives table](https://www.iea.org/reports/electricity-2026/grids)

## Security map

The liquid global-proxy scan includes Siemens Energy AG (ENR, Xetra), GE Vernova (GEV, NYSE) and Eaton (ETN, NYSE). ENR is the selected research proxy because its Grid Technologies reporting directly includes HVDC projects and a €42bn backlog, but reporting remains an aggregate products-and-solutions segment. [Siemens Energy Q4 FY2025 release](https://assets.siemens-energy.com/dam/cefb2e68-55c7-4d86-a913-b3950053facc/Earnings-Release-Q4-FY25-pdf_Original%20file.pdf)

## Equity capture

The required bridge is incomplete: constrained units → ENR product revenue → incremental gross profit → working capital/capex/interest/tax → free cash flow → fully diluted shares. The annual report supplies group free cash flow, but does not isolate transformer/HVDC-converter product revenue, cash conversion or per-share economics. Therefore no product-level rent-capture claim is made and the hard flag `no_material_revenue_bridge` is active. [Siemens Energy FY2025 annual report](https://assets.siemens-energy.com/dam/32b27e9a-af91-4915-8b51-b3af012d0d98/A-Siemens_Energy_Annual_Report_2025-pdf_Original%20file.pdf)

## Three clocks

Scarcity: P10/P50/P90 = 12/30/48 months. Monetisation lag: 18 months. Market discovery: 6 months. The 12-month P50 monetisable runway is structurally adequate, but is not enough to overcome the missing per-share bridge.

## Valuation

No current market-implied base case is asserted because a verified product-level cash-flow bridge and valuation baseline were not available in the admissible evidence. Scenario inputs are deliberately non-underwriting placeholders: bear −30% (30%), base 0% (50%), bull +25% (20%); weighted return −4%. They are not a price target or order instruction.

## Catalysts

Monitor the next segment backlog/order disclosure, product-level margin or cash-conversion disclosure, independent lead-time surveys, and announced factory commissioning milestones.

## Red team

Factory expansions, workforce additions, reconductoring and grid-enhancing technologies may release the constraint; permitting may remain the true bottleneck. Even if equipment pricing stays strong, the listed proxy may retain only normal project margins after capex, working capital, customer bargaining and dilution.

## Kill switches

1. Two consecutive independent quarterly surveys show global large-transformer procurement lead times below 18 months before a product-level bridge appears.
2. The selected proxy reports grid-equipment backlog down at least 20% year over year because of cancellations or price concessions.

## Portfolio fit

No position is proposed. The three screened proxies have zero portfolio weights. Their causal overlap is high: ENR–GEV 0.846, GEV–ETN 0.846, ENR–ETN 0.692, principally through grid capex, electrification, utilities, capacity response, permitting and valuation. Treat them as one root-driver exposure, not diversification.

## Open questions

- Product-level revenue, margin, capex and working-capital allocation for large transformers and HVDC converters.
- Fully diluted per-share linkage and contract price-reset/cancellation terms.
- Independent, current global capacity and lead-time normalization data.
- A current valuation/consensus baseline that can test whether scarcity is already priced.

## Sources

Claim-level URLs, source tiers, independence groups, locators, limitations and documented negative-search results are preserved in `evidence_json` (C-001 through C-006). Source cutoff: 2026-07-23.

Limitations: IEA lead-time evidence is a survey synthesis rather than a capacity census; public segment disclosures do not provide a product-level revenue-to-FCF-to-diluted-share bridge; valuation/consensus baseline is intentionally unresolved; no broker authentication, order action, runtime installation, or Skill modification occurred.
